title: Climate Risk, Adaptation, and Resilience
text:
The Sustainable Advantage: Build lasting impact through sustainability

You are subscribed to BCG's The Sustainable Advantage newsletter.

Manage Subscriptions

Our Approach to Climate Risk, Adaptation, and Resilience
BCG empowers organizations to move from reactive risk mitigation to strategic climate resilience—embedding sustainability into decision-making, strengthening systems, and exceeding regulatory expectations. We bring trusted partnership, sector-specific insight, and advanced analytics to support governments and companies alike.For the Public SectorWe help governments and international organizations define climate resilience as a national priority—and deliver it at scale.Benchmark plans, raise ambition. Assess local and national adaptation strategies using BCG’s proprietary framework and global best practices.Clarify climate risks and impacts. Deploy advanced analytics to model physical risk across social, economic, and natural systems.Make the case for action. Quantify the cost of inaction and define a bankable pipeline to secure adaptation and resilience financing.Align stakeholders. Partner across government, civil society, and industry to accelerate implementation and ensure inclusive climate protection.For the Private SectorWe help companies turn climate risk into competitive advantage—fortifying operations, protecting assets, and upholding responsible business practices. When companies treat regulatory and societal expectations as strategic inputs, they unlock cost efficiencies, revenue growth, and long-term value.Map exposure. Identify physical climate risks, such as extreme heat and flooding, across geographies and facilities.Quantify impact. Assess how risk translates to operational disruption and financial loss down to EBITDA effects.Prioritize response. Identify and sequence adaptation measures based on risk severity, feasibility, and business value.Advance collective resilience. Partner with public actors and peers to shape systemic solutions and co-invest in resilient infrastructure.

The ability of societies and corporates to thrive in the future hinges upon today’s actions. BCG works with governments and international organizations to mitigate the harshest impacts of climate change—and with private sector leaders to build climate resilience while remaining competitive in a low-carbon economy.

Our Client Work in Climate Risk, Adaptation, and Resilience

Our climate risk consulting experts developed an analytical approach to assess impact across the broad span of climate risks to the assets of a main roads' infrastructure operator, informing strategic considerations for the most-relevant areas for resiliency intervention.

Our climate adaptation consulting experts supported a Southeast Asian city to identify priority actions to deliver resilience against a range of anticipated sea-level rise scenarios. Different solutions were assessed from a social, economic, and natural asset perspective to provide a rigorous analytical fact base for both the development of project solutions and the design of an effective national adaptation plan.

Our climate resilience consulting experts supported a megacity government in West Africa to enable data-driven decision-making for the bolstering of its climate resilience by determining the cost of climate inaction in terms of population affected, damage to infrastructure, and impact on the city’s GDP. Based on this, an Adaptation and Resilience (A&R) project pipeline was developed with financing from both the public and private sectors.

Our climate risk assessment experts reviewed a major developed country’s official A&R strategies to both determine the best starting point and assess gaps against best practices and then designed an approach and timeline of action required to develop a national adaptation plan within a short timeframe.

Client Impact

How Michigan Is Turning Economic Transition into Community Resilience

BCG supported the State of Michigan in standing up a new Community & Worker Economic Transition Office to address shifts in the utility and auto sectors, which together make up more than 20% of the state’s economy.

Learn More

Client Impact

Redesigning Banking for the Energy Transition: Societe Generale

Sustainability isn't about ideology. It's about strategy and business. Societe Generale became a leader in ESG by transforming the way it does business, rethinking the value-add for clients, and examining the risks it was willing to take.

Learn More

Client Impact

Japan’s $1 Trillion Leap: Decarbonizing Industry, Empowering Innovation

With support from BCG, the government of Japan is accelerating its net zero ambition—partnering across industry and civil society to drive climate impact, industrial renewal, and global technology leadership.

Learn More

Client Impact

Transforming a Region into a Clean Energy Innovation Hub

BCG experts helped launch a clean energy tech hub with $80 million+ in funding—driving innovation and jobs in underserved communities.

Learn More

Client Impact

How Autostrade per l’Italia Is Building Infrastructure for the Future

Alberto Milvio, former CFO of Autostrade per l’Italia Group, and Concetta Testa, head of sustainability, share how the company is working to realize sustainable infrastructure.

Learn More

Our Partnerships in Climate Risk, Adaptation, and Resilience

UN High-Level Climate Change Champions
As part of our collaboration with the UN Climate Change High-Level Champions, BCG has conducted in-depth research to define the challenges and requirements for achieving the Sharm el-Sheikh Adaptation Outcomes by 2030. Drawing on global experience across public and private sectors, our reports outline what’s needed to meet bold—but actionable—goals in four critical areas of climate resilience.

Harnessing the Private Sector for a Climate-Resilient World

The private sector can be instrumental in building a climate-resilient future through vital systems such as food and agriculture, health, and infrastructure.

Learn More

2030 Adaptation Outcomes for Adaptation and Resilience Finance

There is an adaptation and resilience financing gap that needs to be met. Enabling these flows requires a fundamental transformation of the financial system.

Learn More

2030 Adaptation Outcomes for Adaptation and Resilience Planning

Only ~20% of country governments are planning the necessary A&R actions. Stakeholders need to come together now to plan, fund, and implement them.

Learn More

2030 Adaptation Outcomes for Food and Agriculture

We are on the brink of a catastrophic and multifaceted food crisis. Systemic transformation is needed to both fortify against climate impacts and reduce emissions.

Learn More
